commentaries.author: George Leo Haydock
commentaries.content: 1 Esdras Wisdom of Solomon Wisdom of Sirach Epistle of Jeremiah Prayer of Manasseh Only rebel not against the LORD, neither fear you the people of the land; for they are bread for us: their defense is departed from them, and the LORD is with us: fear them not. To eat, or consume them, as easily as we devour a piece of bread. The expression is proverbial, Psalm xiii. Hebrew, "their shadow "which is taken in the same sense. Septuagint, "their time or opportunity is gone. "The Rabbins refer this to holy Job, who, they say, died at this time. (Cornelius a Lap ide) He dwelt near the Jordan. (Pineda in Job.

methodology.step_1: Parse main page with BeautifulSoup for structure
methodology.step_3: Use Firecrawl to get full content from 'Go to Commentary' links
methodology.step_4: Fallback to BeautifulSoup for accordion content if needed
advantages: Records verses even without commentaries for complete tracking
